volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Casina (Section 2.6.390b - Section 2.7.430)

sections.label: Section 2.6.390b
sections.translation: Aye , faith , that you may hang up by the feet .

sections.label: Section 2.6.391
sections.original: At tu ut oculos emungare ex capite per nasum tuos .
sections.translation: Aye , that you may blow your eyes out of your head through your nose .

sections.label: Section 2.6.392
sections.original: Quid times ? paratum oportet esse iam laqueum tibi .
sections.translation: What are you afraid of ? It must be ready by this — ( Turning to OLYMPIO .) A halter for you , I mean .

sections.label: Section 2.6.393
sections.translation: You ’ re undone !

sections.label: Section 2.6.393b
sections.translation: Give attention , both of you .

sections.label: Section 2.6.393c
sections.translation: I ’ ll be mum .

sections.label: Section 2.6.393d
sections.translation: Now you , Cleostrata , that you may not say that anything has been done cheatingly by me in this matter , or suspect it ,

sections.label: Section 2.6.395
sections.translation: I give you leave , do you yourself draw the lots .

sections.label: Section 2.6.395b
sections.translation: You are ruining me .

sections.label: Section 2.6.395c
sections.translation: He ’ s gaining an advantage rather .

sections.label: Section 2.6.396
sections.translation: You do what ’ s fair .

sections.label: Section 2.6.396b
sections.original: Deos quaeso — ut tua sors ex sitella effugerit .
sections.translation: I pray the Gods that your lot say run away out of the urn .

sections.label: Section 2.6.397
sections.original: Ain tu ? quia tute es fugitivos , omnes te imitari cupis ?
sections.translation: Say you so ? Because you are a runaway yourself , do you wish all to follow your example ? I wish , indeed , that that lot of yours , as they say that of the descendants of Hercules [*] once did , may melt away while the lots are drawing .

sections.label: Section 2.6.400
sections.original: Tu ut liquescas ipse , actutum virgis calefactabere .
sections.translation: And you , that you may melt away yourself , and just now be made hot with twigs .

sections.label: Section 2.6.401
sections.translation: Attend , will you , to the business in hand , Olympio !

sections.label: Section 2.6.401b
sections.translation: Yes , if this thrice-dotted [*] fellow ’ ll let me .

sections.label: Section 2.6.402
sections.original: Quod bonum atque fortunatum mihi sit .
sections.translation: May this prove lucky and fortunate to me .

sections.label: Section 2.6.402b
sections.translation: Yes indeed ; to me as well .

sections.label: Section 2.6.403
sections.translation: Not so .

sections.label: Section 2.6.403b
sections.translation: By my troth , yes , I say .

sections.label: Section 2.6.403c
sections.translation: By my troth , yes , for myself , I say .

sections.label: Section 2.6.403d
sections.translation: He ’ ll be the winner ; you ’ ll live in wretchedness .

sections.label: Section 2.6.404
sections.original: Percide os tu illi † hodie . age , ecquid fit ? ne obiexis manum .
sections.translation: Do you give him a punch in the face this instant ! Well , what are you about ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.405
sections.translation: With clenched or open hand am I to strike him ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.405b
sections.translation: Do just as you please .

sections.label: Section 2.6.405c
sections.translation: There ’ s for you , take that !

sections.label: Section 2.6.406
sections.translation: What business have you to touch him ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.406b
sections.translation: Because my Jupiter ( pointing to STALINO ) commanded me .

sections.label: Section 2.6.407
sections.translation: Do you slap him in the face in return .

sections.label: Section 2.6.407b
sections.translation: I ’ m being murdered , I ’ m being punched with his fists , Jupiter !

sections.label: Section 2.6.408
sections.translation: What business had you to touch him ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.408b
sections.translation: Because this Juno of mine ( pointing to CLEOSTRATA ) ordered me .

sections.label: Section 2.6.409
sections.original: Patiundum est , siquidem me vivo mea uxor imperium exhibet .
sections.translation: I must put up with it , since , as long as I live , my wife will have the mastery .

sections.label: Section 2.6.410
sections.original: Tam huic loqui licere oportet quam isti .
sections.translation: He ( pointing to CHALINUS ) ought to be allowed to speak as much as that fellow .

sections.label: Section 2.6.410b
sections.translation: Why by his talk does he occasion me an unlucky omens

sections.label: Section 2.6.411b
sections.translation: I think , Chalinus , you should be on your guard against a mishap .

sections.label: Section 2.6.412
sections.translation: Full time , after my face has been battered !

sections.label: Section 2.6.412b
sections.translation: Come , wife , now then draw the lots . ( To the SERVANTS .) Do you give your attention . ( To CLEOSTRATA .) And give it , you , as well .

sections.label: Section 2.6.414
sections.original: perii , cor lienósum , opinor , habeo , iam dudum salit ,
sections.translation: Where I am I know not . [*] I ’ m undone , I ’ ve got my heart full of maggots , I think ; it ’ s jumping about already ;

sections.label: Section 2.6.415
sections.translation: with its throbbing it beats against my breast .

sections.label: Section 2.6.415b
sections.translation: I ’ ve got hold of a lot .

sections.label: Section 2.6.415c
sections.translation: Draw it out , then .

sections.label: Section 2.6.416
sections.translation: Are you not dead now ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.416b
sections.translation: Show it . ( She shows it .) It ’ s mine .

sections.label: Section 2.6.416c
sections.translation: Really this is an unlucky mishap .

sections.label: Section 2.6.417
sections.translation: You are beaten , Chalinus .

sections.label: Section 2.6.417b
sections.translation: Then I ’ m glad that we are to survive after all , Olympio .

sections.label: Section 2.6.418b
sections.original: Pietate factum est mea atque maiorum meum .
sections.translation: Through my own piety and that of my forefathers has it happened .

sections.label: Section 2.6.419
sections.original: Intro abi , uxor , atque adorna nuptias .
sections.translation: Wife , go in-doors and make ready for the wedding .

sections.label: Section 2.6.419b
sections.translation: I ’ ll do as you bid me .

sections.label: Section 2.6.420
sections.original: Scin tu rus hinc esse ad villam longe quo ducat ?
sections.translation: Do you know that it ’ s to a distance in the country , at the farm-house , that he is to take her ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.420b
sections.translation: I know .

sections.label: Section 2.6.421
sections.original: Intro abi et , quamquam hoc tibi aegre est , tamen fac accures .
sections.translation: Go in-doors , and although this is disagreable to you , still take care and attend to it .

sections.label: Section 2.6.421b
sections.translation: Very well . ( Goes into the house .)

sections.label: Section 2.6.422
sections.original: Eamus nos quoque intro , hortemur ut properent .
sections.translation: Let us , as well , go in-doors ; let ’ s entreat them to make all haste .

sections.label: Section 2.6.422b
sections.translation: Am I delaying at all ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.423
sections.original: Nam praesente hoc plura verba fieri non desidero .—
sections.translation: For in his presence ( pointing to CHALINUS ) I don ’ t want there to be any further conversation . ( They go into the house .)

sections.label: Section 2.7.424
sections.original: Si nunc me suspendam , meam operam luserim
sections.translation: If now I were to hang myself , I should be losing my pains ,

sections.label: Section 2.7.425
sections.original: et praeter operam restim sumpti fecerim
sections.translation: and besides my pains , putting myself to the expense of purchasing a rope , and doing a pleasure to my evil-wishers . What need is there for me , who , indeed , am dead even as it is ? At the lots I ’ m beaten ; Casina ’ s to be married to the bailiff . And this now is not so much to be regretted , that the bailiff has got the better ,

sections.label: Section 2.7.430
sections.original: quam id expetivisse opere tam magno senem ,
sections.translation: as the fact that the old man so vehemently desired that she shouldn ’ t be given me , and should marry him . How frightened he was , how in his misery he did bustle about , how he did caper about after the bailiff had won . By-the-bye , I ’ ll step aside here ; I hear the door opening .
